raw_consumer_statement: American Express has always been a good company to me for over a dozen years and have never had any problems until recently. My wife 's card information was stolen on XX/XX/2023 card was stolen and charged XXXX on XXXX. Then the next day XX/XX/2023 another three charges were made XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  for XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX, XXXX for XXXX, and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  American Express called and informed me of these fraud charges. All of these charges were removed as fraud except for the XXXX. We disputed this transaction repeatedly and even filed a police report about this fraud charge. All other fraud charges were removed except for XXXX charge. American Express claimed that used correct zip code, so it wasn't fraud. I am unable to verify exactly what happened as it was my wife 's card, but I know she didn't make these charges as she doesn't buy things on the internet as she can't see to do so ( she is visually impaired and it is possible that someone could of stolen her card details ). She really only makes charges in person and American express knows this with her history of purchases. Usually she is with me when her purchases are made. She did not make this charge and American Express claim of 100 Percent fraud protection is just a lie.
